headline: Why The Narrative Around Walt Disney (DIS) Is Shifting As Fair Value And IP Debates Collide
summary: Disney’s fair value estimate currently sits at US$130.57 per share, close to a US$130 price target cited in recent research, which keeps the stock’s valuation debate centered around a fairly tight range. That cluster of targets reflects the tension you see in analyst commentary, with some leaning into Disney’s intellectual property strength and early fiscal year tone, while others trim targets and emphasize execution risk. As you read on, you will see how these differing views shape the...

headline: 'Big Short' Legend Steve Eisman Calls Nvidia's 73% Revenue Growth 'Incredible'
summary: Steve Eisman called it “incredible” that Nvidia Corp grew revenue 73% in a single year, but the stock closed down anyway. On the Real Eisman Playbook podcast the investor, famous for predicting the 2008 crisism walked through the numbers: fiscal Q4 revenue of $68.1 billion, EPS up 82%, and guidance that beat by a wide margin. “Revenue beat, EPS beat, guidance beat by a lot,” Eisman said. headline: Tobacco-Free Kids: Over 160 Organizations Call on Formula 1 to End All Tobacco Sponsorships and Stop Promoting Addiction to Kids
summary: As the Formula 1 racing season starts this week, over 160 public interest organizations from around the world today sent a letter to Formula 1 calling on the sport to update its prohibition of tobacco product sponsorships to include nicotine pouches and stop helping the tobacco industry market its products to F1's millions of young fans.

headline: Disney Credit Move Highlights Liquidity, Valuation Gap And Leadership Transition
summary: Disney (NYSE:DIS) has entered into new and amended credit agreements totaling US$9.25b. The facilities are arranged with major banks and are structured to support liquidity and funding capacity. The refinancing excludes certain higher risk ventures, focusing the agreements on Disney’s core operations. The move comes as Disney prepares for leadership changes, including the planned departures of its chief executive and chief communications officer. For you as an investor, the focus is on what...

headline: Disney launches its Adventure cruise ship — a new foothold in Southeast Asia
summary: The Disney Adventure sets sail from Singapore this month. The company's global aspirations coincide with a sharp decline in international visitors to the U.S.

headline: Disney bets big on the Asian market with Adventure cruise ship
summary: Disney cruises is going bigger than ever with the launch of the 6,700-passenger Disney Adventure, its largest ship and the first to set sail from the Asian market. The ship will embark on three- and four-night voyages from Singapore starting later this month and features immersive experiences inspired by Marvel, Frozen, Toy Story and more. CNBC's Sarah Whitten breaks down Disney's cruise expansion and its global aspirations for its Experiences business.
